contracts.name: DeltaVerifier
contracts._adminNote: DeltaVerifier DEFAULT_ADMIN intentionally stays at the 2-of-3 admin Safe (not the timelock) so mint-config posture changes (attesters, budget, genesis) are not 48h-delayed. Deployer is still revoked. Must match mainnet-launch-posture.json roleAudit.
contracts._submitterNote: SUBMITTER_ROLE goes to the backend relayer (RELAYER = governance.submitterRelayer / MAINNET_BACKEND_ADDRESS), NOT DEPLOYMENT_ROLE which resolves to the bootstrap deployer. Pairing this with SUBMITTER_ROLE in revokedFromDeployer makes the handoff grant the relayer and revoke the deployer in one pass — otherwise the handoff re-grants the deployer's bootstrap SUBMITTER (mainnet 2026-07-01 incident, required a post-handoff Safe cleanup).

contracts.name: DataContributionRegistry
contracts._adminNote: DataContributionRegistry DEFAULT_ADMIN intentionally stays at the admin Safe (not the timelock), consistent with DeltaVerifier and mainnet-launch-posture.json roleAudit. Deployer is still revoked.
contracts._recorderNote: RECORDER_ROLE is DeltaVerifier only (CONTRACT:DeltaVerifier), NOT DEPLOYMENT_ROLE which resolves to [deployer, DeltaVerifier]. With RECORDER_ROLE in revokedFromDeployer the handoff drops the deployer's bootstrap RECORDER while keeping DeltaVerifier's. Same class of bug as DeltaVerifier SUBMITTER (mainnet 2026-07-01). VERIFIER_ROLE stays DEPLOYMENT_ROLE (un-audited deployer-key leftover) — separate follow-up cleanup.

contracts.name: HokusaiToken
contracts._ownerNote: Per-model token Ownable owner stays at the admin Safe (the governor set by the factory at creation, per H-1), NOT the timelock. The token's only owner-power is setController; keeping it with the 2-of-3 Safe (not 48h-timelocked) is the launch decision (2026-06-30). The deployer never owns the tokens, so the handoff does not move them — ensureOwner flags non-deployer-owned contracts instead of reverting; ownership is verified via launch-posture expectedTokenOwner.

contracts.name: HokusaiParams
contracts._rolesNote: HokusaiParams grants DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to its deployer (the TokenManager) and GOV_ROLE to the governor (admin Safe) at construction, and TokenManager exposes no params role-management method — so neither role is the deployer's to move, and GOV_ROLE (the economic-param control: tokensPerDeltaOne etc.) is permanently held by the admin Safe by contract design (consistent with the H-1 token-owner decision: economic/control powers stay under the 2-of-3 Safe, not the timelock). The handoff therefore does not transfer params roles. Moving economic-param control to the timelock would require a TokenManager/params contract change (tracked follow-up).
